title: doughy molten slovakian yeast bake
description: a yeasty, molten, gluten-free-friendly slovakian dish featuring tarragon and a touch of french flavors, perfect for meal prep and served in a mason jar.
instructions: mix the gluten-free flour and warm milk in a bowl until just combined.
add the yeast and let it sit for 5 minutes, or until frothy.
add the tarragon, salt, and vegetable oil, and mix until a smooth batter forms.
pour the batter into a greased mason jar, about 3/4 of the way full.
cover the jar with a clean cloth and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
place the jar on the middle rack of the oven and bake for 25 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the edges are set.
remove the jar from the oven and let it cool on a wire rack for 5 minutes.
use oven mitts to carefully remove the mason jar from the oven and let it cool for a few more minutes before serving.
carefully invert the jar onto a plate and serve hot, with a spoon to scoop out the molten center.
note: if you prefer a more solid center, bake for 30-35 minutes.
